preamble: The merger of the retroflex nasal [ɳ] back into the dental nasal [n], often referred to as dentalization. This is a common feature in many modern Indo-Aryan languages where the phonemic distinction between dental and retroflex nasals has been lost.

examples.note: Loss of phonemic distinction in standard Hindi and many dialects.

sources: Masica, C. P. (1991). The Indo-Aryan Languages.
Fortson, B. W. (2010). Indo-European Language and Culture: An Introduction.
